overallText: One of your results, sodium, was within the typical range. Two others — potassium and hemoglobin — came back a little below their typical ranges. Your provider can help you understand what these results mean together.

perTest.text: Your potassium came back a little below the typical range at 3.2, compared with a typical range of 3.5–5.1 mEq/L. Potassium is an electrolyte that helps control fluid levels and your body's acid-base balance. Talk with your provider about what this result means for you.

Your hemoglobin came back a little below the typical range at 11.2, compared with a typical range of 12.0–15.5 g/dL. Hemoglobin is an iron-rich protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen from your lungs to the rest of your body. Talk with your provider about what this result means for you.
Your sodium was 141, within the typical range of 136–145 mEq/L. Sodium is an electrolyte that helps your nerves and muscles work properly.
